name: Cheniere Energy, Inc.
business: Cheniere Energy is a Houston-based energy infrastructure company primarily engaged in LNG-related businesses. It operates two natural gas liquefaction and export facilities at Sabine Pass, Louisiana and near Corpus Christi, Texas. The company provides clean, secure and affordable LNG to integrated energy companies, utilities and energy trading companies around the world.
namedCustomers: TotalEnergies Gas & Power North America, Inc.

exposures.sentence: We provide clean, secure and affordable LNG to integrated energy companies, utilities and energy trading companies around the world.

catalysts.event: FERC authorization for CCL Midscale Trains 8 & 9 Project
catalysts.sentence: In March 2025, we received authorization from the FERC under the NGA to site, construct and operate the CCL Midscale Trains 8 & 9 Project

catalysts.event: Positive FID for CCL Midscale Trains 8 & 9 Project
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, our Board made a positive FID with respect to the investment in the development, construction and operation of the CCL Midscale Trains 8 & 9 Project and issued a full notice to proceed with construction to Bechtel under a fixed price separated turnkey EPC contract.

catalysts.event: Substantial completions of Trains 1-4 of Corpus Christi Stage 3 Project
catalysts.sentence: In March, August, October and December 2025, substantial completions of Trains 1, 2 3 and 4, respectively, of the Corpus Christi Stage 3 Project were achieved.

catalysts.event: First LNG production from Train 5 of Corpus Christi Stage 3 Project
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, LNG was produced for the first time from Train 5 of the Corpus Christi Stage 3 Project.

In August 2025, Cheniere announced the execution of a long-term LNG SPA between Cheniere Marketing and JERA Co., Inc. ( JERA ), under which JERA has agreed to purchase approximately 1 mtpa of LNG from Cheniere Marketing on an FOB basis from 2029 through 2050.

catalysts.event: IPM agreement with Canadian Natural Resources Limited
catalysts.sentence: In May 2025, Cheniere Marketing entered into an IPM agreement with Canadian Natural Resources Limited to purchase 140,000 MMBtu per day of natural gas at a price based on the Japan Korea Marker, less fixed LNG shipping costs and a fixed liquefaction fee, for a term of approximately 15 years commencing in 2030.
